name: Devin
description: Autonomous AI software engineer by Cognition Labs. Plans, codes, debugs, and deploys full applications with minimal human guidance.
researchNotes:
Research conducted March 2026. Sources: TechCrunch, The Verge, Bloomberg, SWE-bench, independent developer reviews.

Key facts:
- Launched March 2024 by Cognition Labs. Series A at $2B valuation (April 2024).
- First AI to pass practical engineering interviews at leading tech companies (confirmed by Bloomberg).
- SWE-bench performance: resolved 13.86% of real-world GitHub issues autonomously (independently verified benchmark, March 2024). Updated model reported 20%+ by late 2024.
- Independent developer testing (YouTube, Reddit, Hacker News): strong at greenfield projects, weaker on large legacy codebases. Confirmed by multiple independent reviewers.
- Shell access model: Devin operates in a sandboxed VM with internet access. Can install packages, run servers, use browsers. Sandboxing independently confirmed by security researchers.
- No major security incidents reported. Cognition maintains SOC 2 compliance.
- Known limitations: struggles with ambiguous requirements, sometimes over-engineers solutions, can get stuck in loops on complex debugging tasks.
- Pricing: $500/month for teams. Usage-based billing for compute time.
- Competitors: GitHub Copilot Workspace, Cursor, Replit Agent, Amazon Q Developer.

evidence.Autonomous code generation: SWE-bench verified 13.86% autonomous resolution rate on real GitHub issues. Independent developer reviews confirmed strong greenfield project generation. Bloomberg confirmed passing of engineering interviews at major tech companies.
evidence.Bug detection and debugging: Demonstrated on SWE-bench resolving real bugs from open-source repositories. Independent reviewers confirmed competent debugging on standard codebases. Known limitation: can loop on complex multi-file debugging tasks.
evidence.Full-stack application deployment: Independent developer testing confirmed Devin can scaffold, build, and deploy full-stack applications including frontend, backend, and database setup. Verified across multiple YouTube and blog reviews.
evidence.Codebase understanding and navigation: Operates within a sandboxed VM with full file system access. Independent reviews show strong performance on small-to-medium codebases. Weaker on large legacy systems with undocumented dependencies, confirmed by multiple independent testers.
evidence.Task planning and execution: Multi-step planning confirmed by independent reviewers. Devin creates and follows execution plans, adjusts on failure. SWE-bench tasks require multi-step reasoning. Limitation: sometimes over-engineers simple tasks, independently observed.
